audience_segments: Contest judges deciding whether to install and try Nova plus MIND
Technically curious evaluators who need a bounded evidence trail
channel_constitution.voice: Playful, clear, evidence-governed Nova; pith before explanation.
values: Try the products rather than trust a sales reel
channel_constitution.privacy_stance: Crop or blur local paths, account/session data, unrelated plugins, notifications, and private checkout material.
production_constraints: Use only current captured outputs for claimed runtime behavior
Local render claims require a hash-bound receipt; no upload, portability, Windows 11, or independent-reviewer claim
accessibility_practices: Do not communicate material claims through color alone

case_brief.working_title: Don’t Judge Nova From This Video. Install Her.
case_brief.objective: Make judges install both plugins and begin the Nova tour, using the video as evidence-bounded invitation rather than a substitute for trying the products.
case_brief.viewer_job: Decide quickly whether this is worth a fresh-task install and first hands-on prompt.
case_brief.selected_angle: Open on the captured Mara Vey and Orison result, move immediately to the exact install path, and show coordinated agency under delight, decision, repository orientation, verification, and artifact-production pressure.
case_brief.thesis: Nova + MIND earns trust by giving a judge a fast first experience and by preserving the boundaries of what each captured result actually proves.
case_brief.format: 2:50 narrated product-install invitation with captured outputs and command cards.
constraints: No upload or submission before complete human audiovisual viewing
Final viewing, rights, and publication remain stunspot's authority
The production manifest may not contain a repository URL placeholder
rejected_angles: Feature-tour montage: rejected because it would ask judges to trust coverage instead of install and try.
Final-release victory lap: rejected because publication and submission remain human/external states.
Portability pitch: rejected because cross-host claims are outside the captured evidence.

evidence_ledger.claim: The exact local marketplace commands and fresh-task tour prompt are documented in the repository judge path.
A clean-host authenticated GPT-5.6 Ludis scenario produced Mara Vey, the erased island Orison, two consequential relationships, a dangerous truth, and an opening choice.
A clean-host authenticated GPT-5.6 MIND scenario recommended a quieter proof-centered demo and named a decisive uncertainty.
A clean-host authenticated GPT-5.6 Agentic Coding scenario identified absent Git metadata, named a validation command, and preserved its read-only boundary.
The final clean-host authenticated GPT-5.6 TestForge review returned REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS for the package's evidence-bounded decision, while explicitly withholding release-readiness approval.
A clean-host authenticated GPT-5.6 Nova tour scenario offered four routes without requesting personal information.
Nova coordinated the video build through MIND while stunspot retained intent, product judgment, rights, final viewing, and publication authority.
GitHub reports Stunspot/nova-the-optimal-ai-mind as PUBLIC on main, and the first published remote revision matched local commit 57857aec41854c328200ac5f636d3962365e1251.
The 172.066667-second local final MP4 and matching 43-cue SRT are hash-bound; 27/27 automated checks and three independent quality gates passed.
The exact final filename is public at https://youtu.be/1cqEFrP6FZw; anonymous playability, video and audio stream formats, and opening/midpoint/final-card visual checks passed.
stunspot completed the full audiovisual watch and reported no defect.

promise_contract.viewer: A contest judge who can install both Codex plugins from the checkout and wants fast evidence that trying them is worthwhile.
promise_contract.tension: Is this another polished feature tour, or a product worth installing to test on real work?
promise_contract.image: Mara Vey's captured Orison choice beside an immediate, readable three-command install path.
promise_contract.title_logic: An earned output creates curiosity; the title resolves it by promising an install-first, evidence-bounded trial rather than a feature dump.
promise_contract.opening_receipt: The first screen is a captured clean-host Ludis output with a visible playable premise and choice, followed within seventeen seconds by exact install commands.
promise_contract.payoff: The viewer sees concrete, bounded examples of play, decision support, repository orientation, and skeptical verification, then gets a direct instruction to install both plugins and take Nova's tour.
required_captures: Captured Mara Vey and Orison Ludis output and opening choice
Exact three-command installation card and fresh-task tour prompt
Captured MIND proof-centered decision and reversal condition
Captured Agentic Coding read-only orientation boundary
Captured TestForge REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS evidence review and explicit non-readiness boundary
Accountable-artifact production chain with a separate human authority gate
promise_contract.misunderstanding_risk: Viewers may mistake one captured scenario for universal performance or mistake a build plan for a published final video.
promise_contract.integrity_ruling: video complete and published with submission gates remaining: the exact local MP4/SRT passed their recorded checks, stunspot completed the full watch, and the public URL passed bounded availability/playback checks; final repository evidence, rights/terms, and Devpost submission remain outstanding.

build_artifacts.hook: Meet Mara Vey, Royal Cartographer. Orison was erased from every map; now its bells ring beneath dry fields. Don’t judge Nova from this video—install her.

memory_promotion.rationale: No post-release audience evidence exists; do not turn this one bounded production case into channel doctrine.

next_action: Finalize the public repository evidence, enter the verified repository/video/feedback values in Devpost, complete the authorized attestations, and obtain the green Submitted receipt.
